ambiguity: Class-level muscarinic validation exists, but selective CHRM4 execution remains ambiguous after the emraclidine miss.
evidence_basis: xanomeline-based approval supports muscarinic agonism in schizophrenia, while emraclidine keeps selective CHRM4 translation unresolved.

ambiguity: Acute antipsychotic efficacy is compatible with net DRD2 dampening, but class baggage and disease-modification limits remain unresolved.
evidence_basis: Decades of antipsychotic use establish symptom control relevance for net DRD2 dampening, with clozapine as a permissive but nonselective anchor.
contradiction_conditions_json: ["A clinically credible schizophrenia program demonstrates benefit from sustained net DRD2 activation in the same use case."]
falsification_conditions_json: ["Robust efficacy without net DRD2 dampening becomes the dominant replicated path for acute antipsychotic benefit."]
open_risks_json: ["Class baggage around tolerability and limited disease modification remains substantial."]
